---
title: "5b. Visual Correlation Analysis"
author: "carina peh"
date: "May 13, 2026"
date-modified: "last-modified"

  echo: true
  warning: false
  freeze: true
---

## Overview

A correlation matrix shows the pairwise correlation coefficients between all variables in a dataset. In this exercise I used the Wine Quality dataset to explore relationships between chemical properties of wine.

## Reflections

There are several ways to visualise a correlation matrix in R, each suited for different purposes. `pairs()` gives a quick scatter plot overview, `ggcorrmat()` is useful because it layers in statistical significance automatically, and `corrplot()` is the most visually polished and flexible. Reordering by hierarchical clustering was a useful technique, it groups related variables together, making underlying patterns much easier to read. The significance test overlay also helped distinguish meaningful correlations from noise.

## Getting Started

### Loading packages


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(corrplot, ggstatsplot, tidyverse)
```
:::


### Importing data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
wine <- read_csv("wine_quality.csv")
```
:::


## Building Correlation Matrix — pairs() method

### Basic correlation matrix

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pairs(wine[,1:11])
```
:::

:::

### Lower corner only

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pairs(wine[,2:12], upper.panel = NULL)
```
:::

:::

### Upper corner only

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pairs(wine[,2:12], lower.panel = NULL)
```
:::

:::

### With correlation coefficients

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=768}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
panel.cor <- function(x, y, digits=2, prefix="", cex.cor, ...) {
  usr <- par("usr")
  on.exit(par(usr))
  par(usr = c(0, 1, 0, 1))
  r <- abs(cor(x, y, use="complete.obs"))
  txt <- format(c(r, 0.123456789), digits=digits)[1]
  txt <- paste(prefix, txt, sep="")
  if(missing(cex.cor)) cex.cor <- 0.8/strwidth(txt)
  text(0.5, 0.5, txt, cex = cex.cor * (1 + r) / 2)
}
pairs(wine[,2:12], upper.panel = panel.cor)
```
:::

:::

## Visualising Correlation Matrix — ggcorrmat()

### Basic ggcorrmat

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggstatsplot::ggcorrmat(
  data = wine,
  cor.vars = 1:11
)
```
:::

:::

### ggcorrmat with title

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggstatsplot::ggcorrmat(
  data = wine,
  cor.vars = 1:11,
  ggcorrplot.args = list(outline.color = "black",
                         hc.order = TRUE,
                         tl.cex = 10),
  title = "Correlogram for wine dataset",
  subtitle = "Four pairs are not significant at p < 0.05"
)
```
:::

:::

## Visualising Correlation Matrix — corrplot Package

### Basic corrplot

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
wine.cor <- cor(wine[, 1:11])
corrplot(wine.cor)
```
:::

:::

### Using ellipse method

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-17-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
corrplot(wine.cor, method = "ellipse")
```
:::

:::

### Mixed layout

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
corrplot.mixed(wine.cor,
               lower = "ellipse",
               upper = "number",
               tl.pos = "lt",
               diag = "l",
               tl.col = "black")
```
:::

:::

### With significance test

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-21-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
wine.sig = cor.mtest(wine.cor, conf.level = .95)
corrplot(wine.cor,
         method = "number",
         type = "lower",
         diag = FALSE,
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.srt = 45,
         p.mat = wine.sig$p,
         sig.level = .05)
```
:::

:::

### Reordered by hclust

::: panel-tabset
## The plot


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands-on_Ex05b_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The code chunk


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
corrplot.mixed(wine.cor,
               lower = "ellipse",
               upper = "number",
               tl.pos = "lt",
               diag = "l",
               order = "hclust",
               hclust.method = "ward.D",
               tl.col = "black")
```
:::

:::
